name: Auto Memory Index - sonash-v0
purpose: Master index of all user-home memories for sonash-v0, organized by section (User, Feedback, Project, Reference) with one-line summaries and links to individual files.
notes: Index has no frontmatter (raw markdown only). Canonical counterpart exists but is heavily diverged: canonical has 11 Feedback + 6 Project + 4 Reference entries vs user-home's ~50 Feedback + 15 Project + 7 Reference. Canonical MEMORY.md also carries a stale expertise profile ('Node.js/scripting expert, Firebase comfortable') vs user-home's newer 'No-code orchestrator, 269+ sessions' profile. The canonical index is approximately 200 sessions behind the user-home index — major version drift. User-home references user_os_vision.md which is absent from canonical. Index body itself is a content-bleed risk: Project section is fully SoNash-specific.
portable_elements: Index structure pattern (User/Feedback/Project/Reference taxonomy) is portable. The four-section taxonomy with one-line summaries is a universal organizing pattern.

name: Adoption verdict missing from Creator View
purpose: When a repo is classified as a product (application/framework/tool-demo), Creator View in /repo-analysis MUST include a Section 2b Use-As-Is Verdict alongside pattern extraction.
sanitize_fields: skills/repo-analysis reference (JASON-OS uses different skill set)
notes: SoNash-specific: references /repo-analysis skill (not present in JASON-OS). originSessionId present (ee98d374-71f4-45f3-ab3b-f6bb7e46d583). References Session #278 and GitNexus repo as concrete incident. Content-bleed risk if transplanted as-is — the rule's principle (adoption verdict for product repos) is portable but the skill reference is not. JASON-OS equivalent would be whatever analysis skill handles external repo evaluation.
portable_elements: Core principle (product repos need use-as-is verdict alongside pattern extraction) is universally applicable to any repo-analysis skill. The 5-point verdict structure (install path, fit, blockers, recommended path, bottom-line classification) is portable scaffolding.

name: Agent config file revert hazard
purpose: Warns that spawned agents reading shared config files (e.g., .claude/settings.json) at spawn time may silently overwrite changes made by the main session — always verify shared files after agent edits.
notes: No frontmatter originSessionId. References Session #240 (Go-fixes agent reverting statusline path) as the concrete incident. Mentions statusline path (.claude/settings.json statusline entry) which is project-specific detail but the universal principle (snapshot-at-spawn-time overwrite hazard) is fully portable. This file also exists in JASON-OS user-home with nearly identical content. Candidate for canonical promotion in both projects. No canonical counterpart in SoNash.
portable_elements: Entire principle is portable: spawned agents snapshot config at read time, not at write time. The mitigation pattern (verify after agent edits, make config changes after agents complete) is universally applicable.

name: Agent stalling on large input
purpose: Documents that synthesis subagents reading 16+ findings files silently stall due to context exhaustion; prescribes capping input at 10-12 files per agent and splitting into multiple narrower-scoped agents.
notes: References Session #251 (D1a and D3b agents during repo-analysis). Incident count (4 times) and agent IDs are SoNash-specific context but principle is universal. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with similar content. No canonical counterpart in SoNash. Both projects lack canonical promotion for this high-value platform-behavior finding.
portable_elements: Entire principle portable: 10-12 file ceiling per synthesis agent, split-and-re-synthesize pattern, empty return as telltale signal of context exhaustion.

name: Agent teams and Explore agent operational learnings
purpose: Operational learnings from agent team usage: Explore is read-only, role separation, return protocol, parallel agents for implementation, convergence loops, one team per session limit.
notes: HAS canonical counterpart. Canonical version (Session #225 era, 6 numbered rules) is substantially OLDER than user-home version — user-home has evolved to include 'parallel agents for implementation', 'convergence loops verify implementations', and 'testing should use parallel agents'. originSessionId: 7389d098-40f3-498d-a495-fe6dd68bdf2c. Canonical frontmatter name uses filename slug. CONTENT DRIFT: canonical is a strict subset of user-home; user-home has 3 additional behavioral rules not in canonical. Also references .claude/teams/ directory (teams/audit-review-team.md, teams/research-plan-team.md) — SoNash-specific paths.
portable_elements: All 6 core rules portable. The teams/.claude/teams/ path reference is sanitizable. Parallel-agent-for-impl principle (missing from canonical) is a high-value portable addition.

name: feedback_code_review_patterns
purpose: Code review and PR workflow learnings: never reject as pre-existing, detailed PR descriptions, create PR after session-end, commit all review rounds before pushing, do not create PRs until all work is complete.
notes: HAS canonical counterpart. Frontmatter name uses filename slug (quality issue — should be display name). originSessionId: 7389d098-40f3-498d-a495-fe6dd68bdf2c. References PR #457 as concrete incident. Canonical likely has same content (small file at 1053 bytes). Status 'active' present in frontmatter. User-home includes 'Do not create PRs until ALL planned work is complete' as a named rule — check canonical for presence.
portable_elements: All rules fully portable. No project-specific references beyond PR #457 incident (descriptive context only).

name: Commit hook state files with other commits
purpose: Reminds Claude to include hook state files (.claude/state/, override-log.jsonl, docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md) in commits because they accumulate silently and get forgotten.
sanitize_fields: file path list (.claude/state/ contents differ per project)
notes: No canonical counterpart. References SoNash-specific state file paths (.claude/state/hook-warnings-log.jsonl, learning-routes.jsonl, review-metrics.jsonl, docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md) — these paths are project-specific but the pattern (don't let state files pile up, include in any commit) is portable. Also exists in JASON-OS with same principle but different specific file list. The sanitize_fields list differs between projects. Content-bleed risk in file-path list section.
portable_elements: Core principle (state files accumulate silently across sessions, must be included in commits) is portable. The pattern of listing affected files by category is a portable documentation format.

name: Convergence loops are mandatory — never default to single-pass
purpose: Mandates internal convergence loops on every significant pass of work; documents cascade failure from skipping in SWS re-eval; prescribes min 2 iterations and VERIFY-not-TRUST disposition.
notes: HAS canonical counterpart. User-home and canonical are near-identical in content (both reference SWS re-eval 2026-03-14, same 5 cascade errors, same user quote). Minor formatting difference: canonical uses multi-line YAML description value; user-home uses single-line. Status 'active' in both. This is one of the few user-home + canonical pairs where content is genuinely in sync. SWS reference is a SoNash-specific project name but does not change portability of the rule.
portable_elements: Entire rule portable. SWS name in why-section is contextual flavor only — does not affect the behavioral prescription.

name: Deep-plan discovery process for hook audits
purpose: Specifies 5-layer parallel discovery process for deep-plans involving hooks, scripts, pipelines, or infrastructure: per-subsystem explorers, warning/logging infra, skip reasons, ground-truth verifier, execution verifier.
notes: HAS canonical counterpart. User-home and canonical are nearly identical — canonical is a reformatted version of the same content with multi-line YAML description. Both reference /hook-ecosystem-audit as a target skill to update. The /hook-ecosystem-audit skill reference is SoNash-specific (not in JASON-OS) but is sandboxed in 'How to apply' — does not affect portability of the 5-layer framework itself. Status 'active' in both.
portable_elements: The 5-layer parallel discovery framework is fully portable. The /hook-ecosystem-audit reference in the apply section can be replaced with the equivalent skill name in the target project.

name: Deep-plan Q&A format for ad-hoc clarifying questions
purpose: Requires Claude to use the deep-plan skill Q&A format (batched, recommended defaults, phase markers) for any non-trivial clarifying questions — not just formal /deep-plan invocations.
notes: No canonical counterpart. originSessionId: d34c4cb2-3d2e-4db8-9636-b733228f3195. References .claude/skills/deep-plan/SKILL.md and REFERENCE.md — path is universal across projects using the skill. Inline Related memories section links feedback_workflow_chain.md, feedback_deep_plan_research_check.md, feedback_no_unnecessary_brainstorming.md. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with near-identical content. Largest of the 21 files (2556 bytes). The user quote (2026-04-10, T30 Q&A session) is SoNash-specific context but does not affect portability. Guardrail #3 reference is CLAUDE.md-scoped but portable.
portable_elements: Entire behavioral rule portable. Skill path reference (.claude/skills/deep-plan/) is universal convention. The Q&A format elements (batching, defaults, inter-batch synthesis, phase markers) are portable regardless of project.

name: Deep-plan Phase 0 research check is MANDATORY
purpose: Mandates that /deep-plan Phase 0 Step 3 always checks .research/<topic-slug>/ for prior research and offers /deep-research first — corrected 3 times in Session #235.
notes: No canonical counterpart. References Session #235 (3 consecutive /deep-plan invocations all skipped Phase 0 Step 3). Session reference is SoNash-specific but the rule is universal. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with identical content. The 4-step prescription (.research/<topic-slug>/ check, offer to inject, offer to run, no rationalization) is pure methodology. Both projects lack canonical promotion.
portable_elements: Entire 4-step prescription portable. .research/<topic-slug>/ path convention is universal across projects using the deep-research skill.

name: Deep-research formula insufficient for large repos
purpose: Documents that D+3+floor(D/5) agent allocation formula is a floor, not the answer; prescribes presenting both formula output and scope-adjusted recommendation to user.
notes: No canonical counterpart. References Session #235 (formula suggested 15, actual 35) and Session #244 (formula 13, recommended 16, actual 33). Session numbers are SoNash-specific context. The 5-step prescription (compute formula, assess scope, factor verification/challenge/gap-fill, present both numbers, user decides) is fully portable. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with identical content. Both projects lack canonical promotion.
portable_elements: Full 5-step allocation procedure is portable. Gap-fill rounds, verification agents, challenge agents as distinct layers beyond formula baseline — all portable.

name: Deep-research Phases 3-5 are MANDATORY
purpose: Prohibits skipping deep-research Phases 3-5 (CL verification, cross-model Gemini checks, self-audit, presentation with routing menu) — Session #235 scrapped 179 claims after skipping.
notes: No canonical counterpart. References Gemini CLI as cross-model verification tool — external dependency but optional (if unavailable, graceful degradation). Session #235 incident (179 claims scrapped) is SoNash-specific context. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with identical content. Both projects lack canonical promotion. The core prescription (track skill Phase structure, not agent wave numbering; re-read skill at phase transitions) is portable regardless of which cross-model tool is available.
portable_elements: Phase-tracking discipline (skill phases not wave numbers) is portable. CL verification, self-audit, and routing menu presentation are portable. Gemini CLI reference is portable with graceful-degradation caveat.

name: feedback_execution_failure_recovery
purpose: Failure recovery discipline: stop and diagnose root cause before retrying, verify diagnosis, avoid --no-verify and destructive shortcuts, ask user when blocked.
notes: HAS canonical counterpart. Frontmatter name uses filename slug (quality issue). Status 'active'. User-home and canonical are effectively identical at this size (673 bytes). References Session #219 as evidence for blind-retries cascading — SoNash-specific session number but does not affect portability. Smallest file with a canonical counterpart.
portable_elements: Entire rule portable. No project-specific paths or tool names.

name: Grep lacks intelligence — use structured data where possible
purpose: Three anti-patterns for grep: don't use for analysis (use Read+Think), replace pipelines with git pathspec or JSON+jq, never use for verification (use end-to-end functional tests).
notes: No canonical counterpart in SoNash but canonical has feedback_verify_not_grep.md which covers the verification anti-pattern (anti-pattern #3 here). This file is a superset. Same originSessionId (7389d098-40f3-498d-a495-fe6dd68bdf2c) as feedback_interactive_gates.md and feedback_agent_teams_learnings.md (same session). Also exists in JASON-OS user-home. Supersedes relationship: feedback_verify_not_grep.md (canonical) covers a subset of this file. CONTENT DIVERGENCE: the SoNash user-home version references D1a/D3b agent IDs and Session #251 repo-analysis as the grep anti-pattern discovery context — this differs from JASON-OS version which references CC pre-push gate. Both versions carry Session #233/#244 references.
portable_elements: All three anti-patterns portable. Git pathspec and JSON+jq as grep replacements are universal techniques. Verification = end-to-end + regression guard is universally applicable.

name: Wait for user at interactive gates
purpose: Three-part rule: choice prompts are hard stops, never act on a question you just asked, never interpret ambiguous input as bulk acceptance.
notes: No canonical counterpart. Same originSessionId (7389d098-40f3-498d-a495-fe6dd68bdf2c) as feedback_agent_teams_learnings and feedback_grep_vs_understanding — all three from same session. References coverage audit gate, routing menus, depth gates as specific contexts (SoNash skill vocabulary). References the ack-files incident ('troubling' user quote) and the 'user said A ghost from previous answer bulk-accepted 7 questions' incident. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with near-identical content. The skill vocabulary (coverage audit, routing menus) is partially SoNash-specific but the rules are universal.
portable_elements: All three rules fully portable. Interactive gate vocabulary (choice prompts, bracketed options) is universal. Stop-wait-respond pattern applies to any interactive Claude workflow.

name: Learnings log must always complete
purpose: Mandates that PR review learning entry (AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md) + JSONL record (write-review-record.js) are mandatory deliverables of every /pr-review run — never skipped.
sanitize_fields: Step 6/8 references (pr-review skill version-specific step numbers)
notes: No canonical counterpart. References SoNash-specific artifacts: docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md, scripts/write-review-record.js, Step 6/8 of /pr-review skill. PR #448 R1 is a specific SoNash incident. Content-bleed risk: references specific file paths and step numbers that differ in JASON-OS's pr-review implementation. The MANDATORY-deliverables principle is portable but artifact names are not. Absent from JASON-OS user-home memories (JASON-OS equivalent is feedback_commit_hook_state_files.md which covers a broader pattern).
portable_elements: Core principle portable: learning log + machine-readable record are mandatory pr-review completions. The three-artifact requirement pattern (markdown entry + JSONL + state file) is portable but target paths differ.

name: No agent budgets
purpose: Prohibits budget-framing in multi-agent workflows; allocation formula is planning floor not spending cap; never track 'budget used'.
notes: No canonical counterpart. References Session #245 as the correction incident. Also exists in JASON-OS user-home with nearly identical content. Session number is SoNash-specific context. The rule (no budget tracking, spawn as many as needed, use agents-completed not agents-remaining) is fully portable. Both projects lack canonical promotion for this rule.
portable_elements: Entire rule portable. Progress-reporting prescription (completed + running, never remaining-vs-cap) is universally applicable.

name: No artificial caps on synthesis output
purpose: Prohibits capping candidate presentation in repo-synthesis and website-synthesis outputs — user wants the full picture, not a curated top-N subset.
notes: No canonical counterpart. References SoNash-specific skills (repo-synthesis Phase 2.5 Fit Portfolio, website-synthesis) that do not exist in JASON-OS. Content-bleed risk: rule body cites specific skill names and phase numbers. The underlying principle (user decides what to filter, don't truncate output) is universal but the specific skill references require sanitization. JASON-OS equivalent is broader (feedback_no_artificial_caps.md covers the general principle without skill-specific framing).
portable_elements: Core principle portable: never truncate output with arbitrary caps; organize by tier/type/relevance but show everything. User-as-orchestrator-decides-filtering is universally applicable.

name: No auto debt routing
purpose: Prohibits auto-routing findings to TDMS/MASTER_DEBT without presenting options to user first; user retains authority over what enters debt tracking.
notes: No canonical counterpart. SoNash-specific: the TDMS (Tech Debt Management System) with MASTER_DEBT.jsonl is a SoNash-only infrastructure component. The /add-debt skill exists only in SoNash (or JASON-OS as a ported version). The OPTIONS presentation pattern (track via /add-debt, just note, fix now) is portable but the specific tool chain is not. Absent from JASON-OS user-home memories. NOT portable as written — full sanitization would gut the content. The principle (user decides what enters tracking) is general but without TDMS there's no equivalent trigger context in JASON-OS.
portable_elements: The options-presentation pattern before routing to any tracking system is portable. If JASON-OS adds debt tracking, this principle should be replicated with updated tool names.

name: No autonomous deferrals
purpose: Never defer any work item, plan step, or task without explicit user decision — includes DAS items, plan steps, and session carryover.
notes: No canonical counterpart. References 'DAS items' (Deferred Action Steps — SoNash-specific terminology). Absent from JASON-OS user-home (JASON-OS has no equivalent — this behavioral rule is missing from JASON-OS memory set). The rule (no autonomous deferrals without user decision) is universal. DAS is a SoNash-specific term but the concept is generalizable. Potential JASON-OS import candidate.
portable_elements: Core rule fully portable: user decides deferrals, never Claude. The enumeration of contexts where this applies (plan steps, pr-review items, session carryover) is portable with terminology adaptation.
